question: Q2
title: Temperature Sensor Comparison: Kestrel vs Reference Monitors
research_question: How do Kestrel ambient temperature data at each of the 12 open space sites compare with the weather station at 35 Kneeland St and DEP Nubian Square?

rooftop_thermal_mass.explanation: Rooftop concrete absorbs solar radiation during day and releases heat at night, creating ~4-hour thermal lag

landuse_association.interpretation: More greenspace -> cooler readings relative to reference

wbgt_relationship.note: WBGT never reached OSHA caution threshold despite air temp >90F

key_findings: DEP Nubian is the gold-standard reference (r=0.90, bias=-0.37F, RMSE=3.1F)
WS at 35 Kneeland is unsuitable: rooftop thermal mass creates 4-hour phase lag and 13.5F daily bias swing
Lag-corrected WS correlation jumps from r=0.42 to r=0.998
1.4F urban heat island effect across sites (Castle Square hottest, Mary Soo Hoo coolest)
Greenspace is strongest predictor of temperature bias (r=-0.84, p=0.001)
WBGT never exceeded OSHA caution despite air temp >90F
